background: Suppose the life of a certain security alarm system follows an exponential distribution with a constant failure rate λ = 0.00008 failures per hour.

task.query: What is the probability that a security system will last at least 10,000 hours?
task.answer: The probability that a security system will last at least $10,000 \mathrm{~h}$ is $$ P\{T \geq 10,000\}=e^{-\lambda t} = e^{-(0.00008)(10,000)}=e^{-0.8}=0.4493 $$

task.query: What is the probability that a security system will last at least 10,000 hours given that it has already survived 5,000 hours?
task.answer: The probability that a security system will last at least $10,000 \mathrm{~h}$ given that it has already survived $5,000 \mathrm{~h}$ is $$ \begin{aligned} P\{T \geq 10,000 \mid T \geq 5,000\} & = P\{T \geq 5,000\} \\ & = e^{-\lambda t} = e^{-(0.00008)(5,000)}=0.6703 \end{aligned} $$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
